name: Operations & Algebraic Thinking 5th
description: Numerical expressions with parentheses, prime factorization, and paired number patterns
objectives: Evaluate numerical expressions containing parentheses and brackets
Write simple expressions that record calculations described in words
Interpret and compare numerical expressions without evaluating them
Express a whole number from 2 to 50 as a product of its prime factors
Generate two numerical patterns from rules and relate their corresponding terms

ai.guidelines: Focus on evaluating numerical expressions with parentheses and brackets (order of operations), writing simple expressions from verbal descriptions (like "add 8 and 7, then multiply by 2" as 2 × (8 + 7)), comparing expressions without evaluating them, expressing whole numbers from 2 to 50 as products of prime factors, and generating two numerical patterns from rules to compare corresponding terms. Keep all values whole numbers and results within 0-1000.
